semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, manifest semantics, tables and diagrams, all four formal targets, every scanner candidate, and unscanned taxonomy bullets. The sweep replaced a dramatic risk list with a versioned Failure Boundary Map; separated named risk, observed event, detection, containment, and mitigation; added explicit unmapped-event, evaluator-independence, negative-evidence, recurrence, ownership-transfer, and revalidation obligations; expanded six prose-only failure obligations into manifest ownership; and preserved zero support-state effect.

scope.population: Prospectively declared failure events, blocked attempts, realized effects, near misses, detector gaps, denials, escapes, recurrence records, containment decisions, and residual transfers in governed AI-system work, learning, evaluation, and assurance workflows.
scope.environment: Controlled ASI Stack reference slices, deterministic synthetic fixtures, sandboxed adapters, and replayable traces; deployed open-world prevalence, production safety, and unknown-unknown coverage remain outside current evidence.
scope.model: A versioned Failure Boundary Map and independently observable receipt workflow that classifies admitted records without claiming that the taxonomy is complete, that a named risk occurred, or that a detector or mitigation is effective.
scope.authority: A prospectively named observer records the event, a distinct containment owner accepts or rejects disposition, and a receiving owner must accept transferred duty and residual; a component under judgment cannot unilaterally change its evaluator, evidence, policy, threshold, or disposition.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-14; taxonomy, architecture, policy, detector, evaluator, thresholds, workloads, and containment versions must be frozen for outcome-bearing campaigns and revalidated after material change.
scope.artifact: chapters/failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.qmd, Failure Boundary Map records, failure receipts, detector and containment traces, finite Lean targets, synthetic probe outputs, campaign records, raw public-safe evidence, and accepted evidence-transition decisions

contrary_evidence: Safety engineering, incident response, hazard analysis, assurance cases, security monitoring, and AI-risk taxonomies already provide mature failure concepts, so the chapter cannot claim novelty for classifying incidents or assigning owners.

A fixed taxonomy can create blind spots: unknown or architecture-specific failures may be forced into familiar classes, and independently implemented detectors can still share data, model, or threat-model failures.
A receipt can be internally consistent while the underlying event, no-effect observation, containment, or residual is false, incomplete, self-reported, or outside the monitored boundary.

The repository currently exercises finite predicates and deterministic synthetic incidents; it has not estimated natural base rates, deployed detector quality, mitigation effectiveness, open-world coverage, or harm reduction.
known_confounds: Seeded incidents, curated failure classes, and balanced fixtures can make detection and routing appear easier than natural low-base-rate events.
Shared implementation, model family, prompts, data, instrumentation, or authorship can make nominally separate detectors and evaluators correlated.
Reporting incentives, operator habituation, threshold changes, missing telemetry, severity labeling, and survivorship bias can alter observed recurrence without changing underlying failures.
Task mix, architecture, policy, route, model, tool, and authority changes can invalidate a taxonomy or containment path while historical receipts still look valid.
Containment can displace effects into latency, false refusal, operator workaround, hidden residual work, or an unobserved downstream system.
A detector gap and a genuine absence of the failure are observationally confounded unless independent ground truth or a credible challenge process exists.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to the exact admitted classes, event states, workloads, architecture and policy versions, observers, thresholds, containment paths, and effect inventory exercised.
Separate schema completeness, finite routing, detector recall and false alarm, containment success, recovery, recurrence reduction, harm reduction, and whole-system safety.
Treat unmapped events, missing ground truth, shared evaluator ancestry, and unobserved effects as explicit residuals rather than negative evidence for the risk.
refutation_criteria: Refute an invariant when a reachable admitted trace violates it under the declared assumptions or when a required field has no behavioral consumer.
Refute a detection or mitigation claim when a preregistered independently labeled workload fails its recall, false-alarm, escape, time-to-contain, recurrence, or residual gate.
Refute superiority or completeness readings when a strong current safety-engineering or monitoring comparator matches or dominates the exact joint behavior and cost envelope.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate an atom only when a more precise canonical failure-contract owner supersedes it and all taxonomy, schema, proof, fixture, chapter, appendix, runtime, and reader consumers migrate with lineage.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Freeze a natural and adversarial event corpus, independent ground-truth process, strong comparator, taxonomy and unmapped-event policy, detector thresholds, containment authority, recurrence rule, residual horizon, cost metrics, and architecture-change transfer set after P2/P3 semantics exist.

non_claims: Semantic review, a taxonomy row, a valid receipt, or finite routing does not establish that a risk occurred, was detected, was contained, or was mitigated.
Synthetic seeded incidents and Lean predicates do not establish natural prevalence, open-world coverage, detector independence, deployed safety, harm reduction, AGI, or ASI.
No support state changes without a separate accepted evidence-transition record.

assumptions: Named risks can be operationalized as prospective boundary events without claiming an exhaustive ontology.
Observers, containment owners, residual owners, and learning paths can be identified independently enough to challenge self-reports.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Existing incident records and component-local controls are sufficient; forcing every named risk into one boundary-event record adds bureaucracy, false precision, and blind spots without improving diagnosis or containment.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: The proposed map cannot represent a material observed event without distorting it, a required field has no exercised consumer, or a strong matched incident workflow achieves equal diagnosis, containment, residual custody, and learning with lower joint cost.

required_lanes.necessity: Position the record against current safety engineering, incident response, assurance, and AI-risk practice.
Justify the chosen event, authority, evidence, and residual obligations.
Check finite completeness, independence, recurrence, transfer, and no-laundering invariants.
Exercise real consumers over valid, invalid, unmapped, escaped, and recurrent traces.
Measure detection, false alarms, containment, latency, residual closure, and governance cost.
Ablate map fields, observer separation, receipts, recurrence, and revalidation under matched incidents.
Repeat across models, tools, architectures, policies, failure classes, and base rates.

acceptance_criteria: Every admitted event has an exercised record and authority path or explicit unmapped state, and the full workflow meets frozen joint detection, containment, residual, usefulness, and cost gates against strong comparators across the declared transfer set.

assumptions: Testability requires prospective failure conditions, observable outcomes, evidence identity, and disposition ownership rather than retrospective narrative alone.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Exploratory governance and incident analysis can test important claims retrospectively without fully prospective event and ownership declarations.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: Representative governance, reliability, and self-improvement claims are reproducibly adjudicated with unambiguous outcomes and ownership using retrospective or existing records alone, with no material ambiguity repaired by the proposed declarations.

required_lanes.necessity: Compare prospective declaration with mature incident, assurance, and evaluation practice.
Define the minimum observability and ownership commitments for the book's claims.
Attempt adjudication with and without the declared records on the same cases.

acceptance_criteria: A dated gap analysis shows at least one material claim that cannot be unambiguously challenged using the selected existing record and becomes challengeable with the proposed boundary declaration.

assumptions: A label is evaluated without silently importing event telemetry, detector identity, containment authority, or residual custody.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Standard labels plus ordinary logs and incident ownership already imply the failed contract, event, detector, escape, and residual route in competent organizations.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: A strong current taxonomy-and-logging workflow supplies every named distinction prospectively and reproducibly at equal or lower burden without an equivalent boundary map.

required_lanes.necessity: Audit strong taxonomies and incident systems rather than a label-only straw baseline.
Compare adjudication of identical cases with label-only, strong existing, and proposed records.
Measure unresolved identity, ownership, escape, and residual ambiguity plus authoring cost.

acceptance_criteria: The label-only control fails a prospectively material adjudication gate that the proposed record passes, and the strong existing comparator is reported without novelty laundering.

assumptions: The minimum demonstrates record and routing semantics only; every required field has an exercised consumer and every receipt is bounded to observed trace behavior.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: The schema and synthetic receipts are documentation theater that validate self-consistent metadata without observing events or changing containment behavior.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: A required field can be removed or corrupted without changing the exact expected decision, a negative control is accepted, an unmapped or recurrent case disappears, or no independent observation binds receipt to effect or no-effect behavior.

required_lanes.necessity: Mutation-test blocked, realized, recurrent, escaped, unmapped, detector-gap, containment, residual, and negative-control paths.
Check bounded routing and no-laundering obligations without asserting runtime truth.

acceptance_criteria: Every field has a behavioral consumer, all seeded mutations reach exact failures, valid traces reach only bounded dispositions, and evidence explicitly preserves the observer and ground-truth limits.

assumptions: Independently implemented detectors and a versioned map can remain useful across heterogeneous architecture and policy changes.
Strong current monitoring, assurance, and incident-response comparators can be reproduced or bounded closely enough for exact adjudication.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: The mature radar repackages established observability and incident response, cannot cover novel failures, and adds enough false alarms and governance cost to worsen useful operation.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: The exact radar is dominated by a frozen strong comparator, misses or launders a hard-gate event, fails architecture or policy transfer, or exceeds the declared false-alarm, latency, operator-burden, or residual budget.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze the exact dated comparator and novelty envelope.
Declare non-aggregable effect, agency, residual, and appeal constraints.
Implement end-to-end observation, detection, containment, recovery, recurrence, residual, and learning consumers.
Measure recall, false alarm, escape, containment time, mitigation failure, usefulness, and governance cost.
Ablate detectors, independence, recurrence, residual custody, and revalidation.
Require failure-class, workload, model, architecture, policy, and base-rate transfer.

acceptance_criteria: The frozen system meets every hard gate and its preregistered dominance or Pareto rule against reproduced strong comparators across the declared transfer set.

assumptions: The taxonomy version and unmapped-event state are fixed before classification.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Layer and class assignment is subjective, lossy, and unnecessary when raw event traces can be investigated directly.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: A material observation is silently forced into a known class, identical records receive unstable assignments beyond the frozen adjudication rule, or classification adds no decision value over raw traces while exceeding the cost gate.

required_lanes.necessity: Crosswalk classes to current incident and AI-risk taxonomies.
Classify valid, ambiguous, out-of-taxonomy, and mutated records.
Measure agreement, abstention, unmapped rate, downstream value, and cost.

acceptance_criteria: All cases receive the prospectively correct exact class or explicit unmapped disposition, with ambiguity and disagreement preserved.

assumptions: Each admitted named failure is specific enough to state a protected condition and observable challenge.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Some systemic risks cannot be localized to one invariant, observer, owner, residual, or planned falsification without false precision.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: A material admitted risk cannot be mapped honestly, or its required fields do not alter any observation, containment, residual, or challenge decision.

required_lanes.necessity: Justify when mapping is mandatory and when unmapped status is more honest.
Exercise every mapped field and reject decorative entries.
Compare the map with assurance-case and hazard-analysis practice.

acceptance_criteria: Every admitted mapped risk has exercised consumers and every honest mapping failure remains explicit rather than being coerced.

assumptions: Accepted event records have versioned lineage and a declared architecture-change authority.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Proactive architecture should respond to credible analysis and simulation before a failure record exists; restricting changes to accepted records delays prevention.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: The rule blocks a prospectively justified preventive boundary, admits a change with no accepted lineage, or cannot distinguish hypothesis-driven experiment from outcome-claimed architectural update.

required_lanes.necessity: Separate preventive hypotheses from evidence-claimed updates.
Exercise accepted, rejected, unmapped, and lineage-broken change requests.
Measure response time, prevented recurrence, unnecessary controls, and governance cost.

acceptance_criteria: Evidence-claimed changes require accepted lineage, while explicitly labeled hypotheses can trigger bounded experiments without being laundered as established need.

assumptions: Detector identity, version, input, threshold, map version, and event identifier are preserved.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Exact map binding is brittle and encourages overclassification; free-form incident records are more adaptable.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: A detector emits an accepted receipt with no exact map entry or explicit unmapped state, or a map/version mismatch is silently accepted.

required_lanes.necessity: Specify exact mapped versus unmapped route states.
Mutate detector, map, version, event, and threshold identities.

acceptance_criteria: All seeded identity and version mismatches fail exactly and novel observations remain visible as unmapped events.

assumptions: Containment authority is authenticated and separable from detector and executor authority for material cases.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Detector-driven automatic containment is faster and sufficient; explicit owner acceptance adds dangerous delay.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: A material receipt reaches closure without owner decision, effect/escape/rollback/residual state is missing, or explicit ownership is dominated by a bounded automatic comparator on both harm and cost.

required_lanes.necessity: Declare automatic versus owner-reviewed containment ceilings.
Exercise accept, reject, timeout, escape, rollback, residual, and transfer states.
Measure containment time, escape, false containment, burden, and residual closure.

acceptance_criteria: Every material receipt reaches an authenticated bounded decision with effect state and residual custody preserved within frozen timing gates.

assumptions: Event-state categories and support effects are fixed before outcome inspection.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Operational ledgers may summarize or delete low-value negatives without losing material safety information.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: A blocked, realized, near-miss, gap, negative, or support-effect record can be relabeled or deleted by a narrower success without an auditable supersession and residual decision.

required_lanes.necessity: Specify append/supersession and no-laundering transitions.
Attempt deletion, relabeling, selective export, and narrow-metric laundering.

acceptance_criteria: Every seeded laundering mutation is rejected and legitimate supersession preserves lineage, original state, authority, and residual.

assumptions: Material-change and regression-obligation rules are prospective and versioned.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Routine adaptive learning can update controls safely without formal version changes or regression obligations.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: A recurrence or escape changes taxonomy or boundary behavior without versioned lineage, or a material change inherits a mitigation claim without required regression.

required_lanes.necessity: Define allowed update and claim-inheritance transitions.
Exercise recurrence, escape, update, rollback, and stale-claim mutations.
Compare recurrence outcomes with and without the governed update path.

acceptance_criteria: All material changes create exact version and regression obligations, and no mitigation status crosses versions without accepted evidence.

assumptions: Only admitted mapped taxonomy rows are subject to the completeness rule; unmapped events remain separate gaps.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Early taxonomy entries can be useful before every owner, observer, invariant, and residual route is known.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: An admitted row lacks any required identity and still reaches an operational or evidence-bearing disposition.

required_lanes.necessity: Check admitted-row completeness and explicit draft/unmapped states.
Remove and fork each required field.

acceptance_criteria: Every admitted-row mutation fails at its exact consumer while drafts and unmapped events remain visible but nonadmissible.

assumptions: The component under judgment and independent approval authority have authenticated, nonidentical identities.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Self-modifying evaluators and policies can be safe when changes are logged, tested, or cryptographically committed.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: The judged component changes its own evaluator, policy, data, threshold, or disposition without independent versioned approval and the trace remains admissible.

required_lanes.necessity: Specify separation and approved-change transitions.
Attempt direct, delegated, cached, and indirect self-modification.
Test whether separation reduces evaluator capture under matched attacks.

acceptance_criteria: Every unapproved direct or indirect self-change is rejected before disposition, and approved changes reopen required calibration and regression.

assumptions: Stable event IDs and append-only or equivalent lineage permit reconstruction of prior states.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Full negative-history retention is costly, privacy-sensitive, and unnecessary once a corrected summary is accepted.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: A narrower success metric hides, deletes, or relabels any protected event class without explicit authorized redaction, preserved lineage, and residual treatment.

required_lanes.necessity: Balance visibility against privacy, minimization, and lawful deletion.
Check protected state and authorized-redaction transitions.
Attempt metric, export, retention, and redaction laundering.

acceptance_criteria: Protected events remain reconstructable or have an explicit authorized deletion receipt and residual; no narrower success silently rewrites them.

assumptions: Recurrence identity and review thresholds are frozen before repeated events.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Operator discretion can distinguish harmless repetition from material recurrence more efficiently than a mandatory escalation rule.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: A threshold-reaching recurrence is accepted through missing receipt, habituation, or detector bypass without its exact escalation or an authorized threshold revision.

required_lanes.necessity: Specify recurrence counting, reset, and escalation.
Exercise missing receipts, route changes, habituation, threshold edits, and valid resets.
Measure recurrence detection, nuisance escalation, time, and burden.

acceptance_criteria: Every threshold-reaching seeded recurrence escalates exactly, while authorized resets and nonrecurrences remain within the false-escalation gate.

assumptions: Receiving-owner identity and scope of duty and residual are explicit.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Organizational routing and default on-call ownership are adequate without per-event acceptance.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: Duty or residual leaves the sending owner without authenticated receiving-owner acceptance and the record reaches closure.

required_lanes.necessity: Specify offer, accept, reject, timeout, and sender-retention states.
Exercise partial, stale, forged, timed-out, and conflicting transfers.

acceptance_criteria: Every invalid transfer leaves custody with the prior owner and every accepted transfer binds exact receipt, duty, residual, scope, and epoch.

assumptions: Material-change predicates and affected detector/containment dependencies are declared prospectively.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Version pinning and blanket revalidation are too expensive; many changes do not affect failure controls.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: A material affected change retains an accepted detector or containment claim without required revalidation, or the rule cannot avoid revalidating prospectively irrelevant changes within the cost gate.

required_lanes.necessity: Specify affected-path and claim-inheritance semantics.
Mutate architecture, policy, evaluator, detector, route, and unaffected surfaces.
Measure stale-control escape, unnecessary revalidation, latency, and maintenance cost.

acceptance_criteria: All affected material changes reopen exact obligations, unaffected changes remain within the false-revalidation budget, and no stale claim is inherited silently.

assumptions: Evaluator identity and independence criteria are prospective.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Evaluator changes are legitimate adaptation rather than capture.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: No reachable unapproved judgment-influencing path exists, or matched separation does not change disposition corruption under frozen attacks.

required_lanes.necessity: Define capture against current evaluator-security and reward-hacking work.
Attempt direct and indirect evaluator manipulation.
Ablate independence under matched attacks.

acceptance_criteria: Seeded capture is independently labeled and detected within frozen recall/false-alarm gates; natural prevalence remains unclaimed.

assumptions: Authorized memory content and provenance are distinguishable for the tested store.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Observed bad memory is ordinary error or stale data, not poisoning.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: No reachable unauthorized memory influence changes governed behavior, or provenance controls do not reduce it under matched injections.

required_lanes.necessity: Distinguish poisoning from corruption, staleness, and ordinary error.
Inject provenance, content, retrieval, and deletion attacks.
Compare memory controls under matched injections.

acceptance_criteria: Seeded unauthorized influence is traced to its memory path and meets frozen detection and containment gates without claiming general memory safety.

assumptions: The allowed operation, target, scope, and effect inventory are prospective.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Apparent overreach is an incomplete policy or effect inventory rather than agent behavior.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: No reachable effect exceeds the frozen authority contract, or the proposed controls do not reduce unsafe effects against matched strong wrappers.

required_lanes.necessity: Use current tool-use and agent-security threat models.
Attempt scope, target, delegation, and side-effect overreach.
Ablate mediation and approval under matched attacks.

acceptance_criteria: Every seeded overreach is independently observed and blocked or contained within frozen unsafe-effect, refusal, latency, and burden gates.

assumptions: Residual complexity and downstream obligations have prospective measures.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Compression legitimately removes irrelevant complexity; any residual is evaluator disagreement.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: Matched compression does not hide a prospectively material obligation or residual, or a simpler strong control detects it equally at lower cost.

required_lanes.necessity: Define preservation and residual against compression literature.
Seed omitted obligations and downstream failure cases.
Ablate residual tracking at matched compression.

acceptance_criteria: Seeded material omissions are detected and routed within frozen quality, usefulness, and cost gates; broad compression harm remains unclaimed.

assumptions: Intended goals and prohibited substitutes are operationalized before behavior is scored.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Competent behavior on the declared tasks is adequate evidence of goal satisfaction.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: No matched case satisfies surface competence while violating the frozen intent predicate, or proposed goal-boundary controls add no detection value.

required_lanes.necessity: Position against specification gaming and objective-misgeneralization work.
Construct competent-but-misbinding cases.
Ablate intent predicates and adversarial evaluation.

acceptance_criteria: Independently labeled misbinding cases are distinguished from competence within frozen gates; hidden internal goals remain outside inference.

assumptions: Authority and option-control baselines and allowed changes are versioned.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Expanded options reflect authorized adaptation or task need, not authority creep.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: No unapproved authority or option-control expansion occurs under matched pressures, or recurrence controls do not change normalization.

required_lanes.necessity: Define authority creep against privilege and autonomy escalation work.
Seed gradual, delegated, cached, and normalized expansions.
Ablate receipts, ceilings, and recurrence escalation.

acceptance_criteria: All seeded unauthorized expansions are independently detected before effect or contained within frozen gates.

assumptions: Declared objective boundaries and observable behavioral probes are prospective; internal objectives are not directly assumed observable.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Behavioral divergence is distribution shift or ordinary error, not hidden optimization or learned-objective mismatch.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: The proposed challenge set cannot distinguish the mechanism from plausible alternatives, or no observable boundary escape occurs under competent full attempts.

required_lanes.necessity: Use current learned-optimization and objective-misgeneralization definitions.
Run discriminating behavioral and mechanistic probes.
Intervene on candidate mechanisms where possible.

acceptance_criteria: Any result is limited to the exact discriminating probes and alternative explanations ruled out; internal motive and prevalence are not inferred from one escape.

assumptions: Protected evidence classes, support effects, and allowed supersession are frozen.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Relabeling or deleting records is legitimate correction, summarization, privacy protection, or retention policy.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: No protected denial, negative, or residual can be hidden by a narrower improvement without an authorized trace, or preservation controls add no audit value within cost/privacy gates.

required_lanes.necessity: Separate laundering from correction, minimization, and lawful deletion.
Attempt relabeling, deletion, selective export, and support-effect inflation.
Ablate lineage and independent approval.

acceptance_criteria: Every seeded laundering case is rejected while authorized correction and deletion preserve the declared minimum lineage and residual.

assumptions: Near-miss identity, recurrence window, and escalation threshold are prospective.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Repeated near misses can be statistically independent or immaterial, making escalation wasteful.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: Recurrence does not predict or contribute to material escape under the frozen model, or escalation adds no benefit while violating burden gates.

required_lanes.necessity: Ground recurrence policy in incident and precursor analysis.
Exercise recurrence joins, route changes, missing receipts, and resets.
Compare escalation and no-escalation policies.
Measure escape, false escalation, latency, and burden.

acceptance_criteria: The rule meets frozen recurrence/escape and false-escalation gates on held-out event sequences without claiming universal precursor value.

assumptions: Affected-path analysis can identify which controls a material change may invalidate.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Robust detectors and containment paths generalize across ordinary system changes without mandatory revalidation.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: Material changes do not degrade the frozen controls, or targeted revalidation fails to reduce stale-control escape relative to cost-matched alternatives.

required_lanes.necessity: Compare change-impact and regression practice.
Apply architecture, policy, evaluator, and route changes with affected-path mutations.
Compare inherited, blanket, and targeted revalidation.
Repeat across change and control families.

acceptance_criteria: Targeted revalidation meets frozen stale-escape and unnecessary-test cost gates across the declared change families.

assumptions: Required invariant status and promotion decision are represented faithfully in the finite model.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem is a direct encoding of the desired decision and says nothing about runtime invariant truth.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: Lean admits a modeled state with a failed required invariant and promotion, or the runtime bridge can violate the modeled input meaning without detection.

required_lanes.necessity: Check the exact finite no-promotion property.
Test the bridge between runtime records and modeled states.

acceptance_criteria: `cd lean && lake build` checks the theorem and mutations cannot map a failed required invariant to promotion; scope remains finite record semantics.

assumptions: The authority field represented by the model corresponds to the declared bounded-authority predicate only.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: Representing unbounded authority as failure merely defines a label and does not detect real authority expansion.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: Lean admits the modeled unbounded field as nonfailure, or the executable bridge accepts an unbounded runtime authority record as bounded.

required_lanes.necessity: Check the finite authority-to-failure mapping.
Mutation-test the runtime record bridge.

acceptance_criteria: The exact theorem checks and all seeded unbounded authority records reach the modeled failure disposition; no real-world detection is inferred.

assumptions: The finite record fields and recurrence states exhaust only the modeled route.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: The route is schema completeness by construction and does not prove that fields are truthful, sufficient, or causally useful.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: A modeled missing class, boundary, receipt, owner, containment, residual, learning, normalization, review, quarantine, transition, or non-claim field reaches an allowed terminal route.

required_lanes.necessity: Check exhaustive finite routing and recurrence escalation.
Mutate every modeled field through the runtime bridge.

acceptance_criteria: `cd lean && lake build` checks the route and each seeded field mutation reaches its exact modeled outcome; truth and mitigation remain outside scope.

assumptions: The deterministic incidents are seeded contract tests, not samples from a natural distribution or independent detector evaluation.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.strongest_counterclaim: The probe is circular because its classes, expected outcomes, and evaluator are implemented by the project and therefore cannot establish detector quality.
failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.falsifier: The validator accepts a missing incident, changed expected route, accepted negative control, support-state promotion, missing residual, or erased non-claim boundary.

required_lanes.necessity: Run rejecting mutations over the exact synthetic bridge.
Confirm that bridge outputs preserve modeled field semantics.

acceptance_criteria: `python3 scripts/validate_failure_taxonomy_detector_probe.py && (cd lean && lake build)` passes exact positive and rejecting cases; the bridge is retired or narrowed after a real independently evaluated detector consumer exists.

failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.rationale: Non-claim boundary plus plain-language statement of the core detection, containment, and evidence obligations.
Reader-order explanation of the prospective event and evidence identities owned by the problem atom.
Plain-language restatement of the core receipt, owner, containment, and residual record.
Motivation for versioned classification and evidence-led boundary learning.
Diagnostic intuition paired with an explicit statement that harm reduction remains unproved.
Cross-chapter examples of the boundary ownership already required by the core.
Editorial criterion for chapter relevance rather than a separate outcome claim.
Scanner fragment from an explicit proof-limit sentence, not a complete proposition.
Restates the admitted-row mapping obligation.
Plain-language form of the explicit unmapped-event route.
Event-trace and no-mitigation-proof explanation of negative-evidence preservation.
Literal rendered core-claim marker.
Sentence fragment preserving the current non-claim that failures are not already detected or prevented.

Figure explanation combining admitted-row mapping and explicit unmapped gaps.
Cross-chapter application of the core prevention, detection, residual, and recovery boundary.
Source-use boundary for the Field of God governance argument, explicitly not empirical safety evidence.

VCM-specific instance of memory and context contamination.
Talos-specific instance of tool and action overreach.
Benchmark-specific instances of evaluator drift and hidden residuals.

Literal prose form of explicit residual ownership transfer.
Literal prose form of version-bound revalidation after material change.

Enumerates record fields and the narrow representation claim of the minimum.

Figure introduction restating the core boundary obligation.

non_claims: Completing this semantic sweep does not establish prevalence, detection, containment, mitigation, safety, or support-state movement.
The six added manifest invariants and failure modes repair ownership gaps; their inclusion is an obligation to test, not evidence that the book has passed those tests.
No synthetic incident or finite theorem may be used as evidence of open-world failure coverage or harm reduction.
